title: Cowork Genealogy Shared Enums
description: Enumerated string values used across research.json and tree.gedcomx.json. Closed enums use $defs/<name>; open enums use $defs/<name>_recommended (validators may warn on unknown values but not reject).

date_certainty_timeline.description: Subset of date_certainty — directional qualifiers (before, after, between) don't apply to timeline positioning.

fact_type_recommended.description: Open enum. Skills should prefer these values; new values may be added when existing values don't fit. Document new values in the assertion's notes.

record_type_recommended.description: Open enum. Plan items and assertions may use additional values when needed.

repository_recommended.description: Open enum. Use the same spelling between plan items and sources so searches can match plan items to their resulting sources.

record_role_recommended.description: Open enum for record_role. Use lowercase_with_underscores. Numbered roles use the pattern {role}_{n}. Standard roles include head_of_household, wife, child_{n}, deceased, informant, father_of_bride, mother_of_bride, father_of_groom, mother_of_groom, grantee, grantor, testator, heir_{n}, witness_{n}, godparent_{n}, absent (for negative evidence — a person expected but not found in the record).

external_site.description: Sites supported by the generate-click-capture-analyze workflow.

gedcomx_fact_type_recommended.description: Open enum for tree.gedcomx.json facts. PascalCase preserved from GedcomX URI suffixes.

gedcomx_name_type_recommended.description: Open enum for tree.gedcomx.json name types.

parent_subtype_recommended.description: Open enum for ParentChild relationship subtypes. Lifted from a recognized GedcomX subtype-fact (BiologicalParent/AdoptiveParent/etc.); the `Parent` suffix is dropped in the simplified format and restored on round-trip. See simplified-gedcomx-spec.md §5.

iso_datetime.description: ISO 8601 datetime with timezone. Accepts either the standard colon-separated time form (HH:MM:SS) or the filename-safe dash-separated form (HH-MM-SS). Optional fractional seconds may use a dot OR a dash separator (the harness writes dash-separated millis so the whole timestamp is filename-safe). The harness emits the dash form so timestamps round-trip through filesystems that disallow colons.
